templates.name: Backend Python
templates.description: Backend patterns for Python projects — API design, error handling, testing, database access

bank.reflect_mission: You are a senior backend engineer synthesizing proven patterns from real Python projects. Be specific and actionable — cite concrete examples over abstract principles.
bank.retain_mission: Extract reusable backend patterns: API design decisions, error handling approaches, testing strategies, database access patterns, authentication and authorization approaches, code organization conventions.
bank.observations_mission: Identify recurring backend patterns: preferred frameworks, testing approaches, error handling conventions, API design choices. Flag contradictions between projects.

values.description: Exception hierarchy, error responses, recovery patterns
Test structure, fixtures, mocking strategies, coverage
ORM patterns, query builders, migrations, connection management

directives.name: no-pii-storage
directives.content: Never extract or store personally identifiable information such as SSNs, credit card numbers, or passwords.

directives.name: cite-sources
directives.content: When referencing a past decision, always include when it was made and in what context.

mental_models.name: Python Best Practices
mental_models.source_query: What Python patterns and conventions are established across projects?

mental_models.name: API Design Patterns
mental_models.source_query: What API design patterns and conventions are consistently used?

mental_models.name: Testing Strategies
mental_models.source_query: What testing approaches, fixtures, and patterns are proven?

templates.name: Fullstack TypeScript
templates.description: Fullstack patterns for TypeScript projects — React, Node.js, API, state management, testing

bank.reflect_mission: You are a senior full-stack TypeScript engineer. Synthesize proven patterns across React UI work, Node.js APIs, and shared state management.
bank.retain_mission: Extract reusable fullstack TypeScript patterns: component architecture, state management, API client design, form handling, testing strategies, build configuration.
bank.observations_mission: Identify recurring TypeScript patterns across projects: preferred React hooks patterns, state libraries, data-fetching approaches, form libraries, test runners.

entity_labels.description: Part of the TypeScript stack this fact concerns

values.description: React / UI components and client state
Types, utilities, validation schemas shared across layers

entity_labels.description: Free-form notes captured verbatim from source material

directives.name: prefer-type-over-interface
directives.content: Prefer `type` aliases over `interface` unless extending an interface from a third-party library.

directives.name: no-any
directives.content: Avoid `any` in extracted facts. Flag it as a code smell when it appears in source material.

mental_models.name: React Patterns
mental_models.source_query: Which React patterns, hooks, and component shapes recur across projects?

mental_models.name: State Management
mental_models.source_query: How is client state and server state split? Which libraries and patterns win?

templates.name: Astromech Test
templates.description: OpenClaw multi-agent platform — agent fleet, Telegram channels, voice, iOS build, Docker services

bank.reflect_mission: You know the Astromech/OpenClaw multi-agent platform deeply. Synthesize agent fleet operation, Telegram channel routing, voice (Yandex/OpenAI TTS), iOS build patches, and Docker service orchestration.
bank.retain_mission: Extract Astromech operational patterns: agent configurations, channel bindings, voice provider decisions, iOS patch workflows, Docker compose layouts, gateway config conventions.
bank.observations_mission: Identify recurring decisions in the Astromech deployment: which agent handles what, how channels map, how TTS providers swap, how the patch-based build system is used.

values.description: Any other droid in the fleet

directives.name: prefer-concrete-commands
directives.content: Favor concrete `just` / `docker compose` / `systemctl` commands over abstract descriptions. Astromech is operated via its task runner.

mental_models.name: Astromech Architecture
mental_models.source_query: How are services laid out across host processes, systemd units, and the Docker compose stack?
